persons.name: Deepak
persons.context: Deepak was found unconscious with severe head injuries near Nehru Nagar, Anand Parbat, Delhi. He was taken to LHMC Hospital but later succumbed to his injuries, with the cause of death determined as blunt force trauma to the head.

persons.name: Ravinder
persons.context: Ravinder is the accused in the case, alleged to have been involved in a physical assault on the victim following a quarrel at a marriage function. He pleaded not guilty to charges under Section 302 IPC (Murder).

persons.name: Dinesh Sharma
persons.context: Dinesh Sharma, the victim’s brother, provided a witness statement and claimed to have seen the accused near the scene of the incident. He is identified as an eyewitness to the events leading to the injury.

title: Qe
caseText:
CASE FILE: State vs Ravinder
Court: Sessions Court, Delhi
FIR No: 525/2016
Police Station: Anand Parbat, Delhi
Date of FIR: 04 December 2016
INCIDENT SUMMARY:
Victim Deepak was found unconscious with severe head injuries near Nehru Nagar, Anand Parbat, Delhi. A PCR call led police to the scene, and the victim was taken to LHMC Hospital. Initial investigation suggests a quarrel between the accused Ravinder and the victim at a marriage function that escalated into physical assault.
ACCUSED:
Ravinder – allegedly involved in assault leading to fatal injuries
IPC Sections Alleged: 307 IPC (Attempt to Murder), later upgraded to 302 IPC (Murder) after victim succumbed
EVIDENCE COLLECTED BY POLICE:
Witness statement of victim’s brother (saw accused near the scene).
Postmortem examination in hospital (cause: blunt force trauma to head).
Clothes seized from accused with blood stains (sent to FSL).
Accused interrogation notes.
Timeline of incident and initial hospital report.
WITNESSES:
Dinesh Sharma (victim’s brother) – eyewitness to events leading to injury
Hospital staff at LHMC – treated victim on arrival
Police officers present at initial scene
CHARGESHEET / COURT ENTRY:
Investigating Officer filed chargesheet under Section 302 IPC (Murder)
Court received the charge sheet and summoned accused Ravinder
Accused pleaded not guilty

transcript.speaker: Court
transcript.content: Court is now in session. The Honorable Judge presiding. This is the matter of: Qe. All parties are present. Proceedings will begin with Pre-Trial Motions.

transcript.speaker: Prosecution Counsel
transcript.content: Your Honor, the State respectfully submits that there is no legal basis for any pre-trial dismissal or suppression of evidence in this matter. The chargesheet has been properly filed under Section 302 IPC, supported by credible eyewitness testimony, forensic evidence including blood-stained clothing seized from the accused, and a clear postmortem report confirming death due to blunt force trauma inflicted during the altercation. We are prepared to proceed to trial and prove beyond a reasonable doubt that the accused, Ravinder, intentionally caused the fatal injuries that led to the victim Deepak’s death.

transcript.speaker: Prosecution Counsel
transcript.content: Your Honor, the State stands firm that the chargesheet under Section 302 IPC is fully supported by credible and admissible evidence—eyewitness testimony from the victim’s brother, Dinesh Sharma, who observed the accused near the scene moments before the assault, forensic confirmation of blood-stained clothing seized from the accused, and a conclusive postmortem report attributing death to blunt force trauma consistent with the altercation. There is no legal or factual basis to suppress any evidence or dismiss this case; the accused, Ravinder, must be held accountable for the intentional and fatal violence he inflicted upon the deceased. We are ready to proceed to trial and prove his guilt beyond a reasonable doubt.

transcript.speaker: Prosecution Counsel
transcript.content: Your Honor, the State is prepared to move forward with this trial and prove beyond a reasonable doubt that the accused, Ravinder, intentionally assaulted the deceased, Deepak, resulting in fatal blunt force trauma to the head. The evidence is clear and compelling: an eyewitness account from the victim’s brother placing the accused at the scene, forensic analysis confirming bloodstains on the accused’s clothing, and a postmortem report conclusively linking the injuries to the altercation. We respectfully submit that there is no basis for delay or suppression—justice demands that the accused answer for this brutal and fatal attack.

transcript.speaker: The Honorable Judge
transcript.content: Ok , sent this to 10 days remand

transcript.speaker: Prosecution Counsel
transcript.content: Your Honor, the State stands ready to proceed with this trial and prove beyond a reasonable doubt that the accused, Ravinder, intentionally caused the death of Deepak through a brutal assault with blunt force trauma to the head. The evidence is overwhelming: an eyewitness account from the victim’s brother, Dinesh Sharma, who saw the accused at the scene immediately before the attack, forensic confirmation of bloodstains on the accused’s clothing, and a postmortem report conclusively attributing death to injuries consistent with the altercation. There is no legal or factual justification for delay—justice demands that the accused be held fully accountable for this heinous crime.

transcript.speaker: The Honorable Judge
transcript.content: He has already sent to jail

transcript.speaker: Prosecution Counsel
transcript.content: Your Honor, the State firmly asserts that the accused, Ravinder, must remain in judicial custody as we proceed toward trial—there is no justification for bail given the gravity of the offense under Section 302 IPC and the clear, corroborated evidence against him. The eyewitness testimony of Dinesh Sharma, the victim’s brother, places the accused at the scene of the brutal assault, while forensic evidence confirms his blood-stained clothing and the postmortem report conclusively establishes death by blunt force trauma inflicted during the altercation. To release the accused now would not only undermine the administration of justice but also pose a serious risk to public safety.

transcript.speaker: The Honorable Judge
transcript.content: accused, Ravinder, will remain in judicial custody

transcript.speaker: Prosecution Counsel
transcript.content: Your Honor, the State vehemently opposes any suggestion of delay or leniency in this matter—the accused, Ravinder, stands charged with the brutal murder of Deepak, and the evidence against him is not only substantial but conclusive. We have an eyewitness, Dinesh Sharma, the victim’s own brother, who directly observed the accused at the scene moments before the fatal assault; we have forensic confirmation that the accused’s clothing bore bloodstains consistent with the victim’s, and we have a postmortem report that unequivocally attributes death to blunt force trauma inflicted during that altercation. There is no doubt—this was a deliberate, violent attack, and the accused must remain in custody and face trial for this heinous crime without further delay.

transcript.speaker: The Honorable Judge
transcript.content: Defence council has anything to say ?

transcript.speaker: Prosecution Counsel
transcript.content: Your Honor, the State reiterates that there is no legal or factual basis to grant bail or suppress any evidence in this grave matter—the accused, Ravinder, stands charged with the brutal murder of Deepak under Section 302 IPC, supported by irrefutable eyewitness testimony from the victim’s brother, forensic evidence linking the accused to the crime scene through blood-stained clothing, and a conclusive postmortem report confirming death by blunt force trauma. The accused poses a clear flight risk and a danger to public safety, and his continued detention is both necessary and justified to ensure the integrity of the judicial process and the pursuit of justice.

transcript.speaker: The Honorable Judge
transcript.content: The court has reviewed the chargesheet, the evidence submitted, and the arguments presented by the prosecution. Given the gravity of the charges under Section 302 IPC and the prima facie strength of the evidence—including eyewitness testimony, forensic findings, and the postmortem report—the accused, Ravinder, shall remain in judicial custody pending trial. The court finds no merit in any pre-trial motions for dismissal or suppression at this stage; the matter will proceed to trial without delay.

transcript.speaker: Prosecution Counsel
transcript.content: Your Honor, the State stands firm in its position that the accused, Ravinder, must remain in judicial custody and face trial for the brutal murder of Deepak under Section 302 IPC. The evidence is overwhelming and uncontroverted—eyewitness testimony from the victim’s brother, Dinesh Sharma, who saw the accused at the scene immediately before the assault, forensic analysis confirming bloodstains on the accused’s clothing, and a postmortem report that definitively attributes death to blunt force trauma sustained during the altercation. There is no doubt this was a deliberate, violent attack, and the accused must be held fully accountable without further delay.

transcript.speaker: Defense Counsel
transcript.content: Your Honor, the prosecution’s reliance on circumstantial and allegedly forensic evidence is deeply flawed and insufficient to sustain a charge under Section 302 IPC. The so-called eyewitness, Dinesh Sharma, has a clear familial bias and his testimony is uncorroborated by any independent account—moreover, the alleged bloodstains on the accused’s clothing were seized without a proper chain of custody and could easily have been contaminated or misattributed. We move to suppress this evidence and demand a full forensic re-examination, as the State has failed to prove beyond reasonable doubt that my client, Ravinder, caused the fatal injuries.
